NKJV.1: To the Chief Musician. To Jeduthun. A Psalm of Asaph. I cried out to God with my voice— To God with my voice; And He gave ear to me.
NKJV.2: In the day of my trouble I sought the Lord; My hand was stretched out in the night without ceasing; My soul refused to be comforted.
NKJV.3: I remembered God, and was troubled; I complained, and my spirit was overwhelmed. Selah
NKJV.4: You hold my eyelids open; I am so troubled that I cannot speak.
NKJV.5: I have considered the days of old, The years of ancient times.
NKJV.6: I call to remembrance my song in the night; I meditate within my heart, And my spirit makes diligent search.
NKJV.7: Will the Lord cast off forever? And will He be favorable no more?
NKJV.8: Has His mercy ceased forever? Has His promise failed forevermore?
NKJV.9: Has God forgotten to be gracious? Has He in anger shut up His tender mercies? Selah
NKJV.10: And I said, “This is my anguish; But I will remember the years of the right hand of the Most High.”
NKJV.11: I will remember the works of the LORD; Surely I will remember Your wonders of old.
NKJV.12: I will also meditate on all Your work, And talk of Your deeds.
NKJV.13: Your way, O God, is in the sanctuary; Who is so great a God as our God?
NKJV.14: You are the God who does wonders; You have declared Your strength among the peoples.
NKJV.15: You have with Your arm redeemed Your people, The sons of Jacob and Joseph. Selah
NKJV.16: The waters saw You, O God; The waters saw You, they were afraid; The depths also trembled.
NKJV.17: The clouds poured out water; The skies sent out a sound; Your arrows also flashed about.
NKJV.18: The voice of Your thunder was in the whirlwind; The lightnings lit up the world; The earth trembled and shook.
NKJV.19: Your way was in the sea, Your path in the great waters, And Your footsteps were not known.
NKJV.20: You led Your people like a flock By the hand of Moses and Aaron.

KJV.16: The waters<S>4325</S> saw<S>7200</S> thee, O God<S>430</S>, the waters<S>4325</S> saw<S>7200</S> thee; they were afraid<S>2342</S>: the depths<S>8415</S> also were troubled<S>7264</S>.

ESV.1: I cry aloud to God, aloud to God, and he will hear me.
ESV.2: In the day of my trouble I seek the Lord; in the night my hand is stretched out without wearying; my soul refuses to be comforted.
ESV.3: When I remember God, I moan; when I meditate, my spirit faints. Selah
ESV.4: You hold my eyelids open; I am so troubled that I cannot speak.
ESV.5: I consider the days of old, the years long ago.
ESV.6: I said,  “Let me remember my song in the night; let me meditate in my heart.” Then my spirit made a diligent search:
ESV.7: “Will the Lord spurn forever, and never again be favorable?
ESV.8: Has his steadfast love forever ceased? Are his promises at an end for all time?
ESV.9: Has God forgotten to be gracious? Has he in anger shut up his compassion?” Selah
ESV.10: Then I said, “I will appeal to this, to the years of the right hand of the Most High.”
ESV.11: I will remember the deeds of the LORD; yes, I will remember your wonders of old.
ESV.12: I will ponder all your work, and meditate on your mighty deeds.
ESV.13: Your way, O God, is holy. What god is great like our God?
ESV.14: You are the God who works wonders; you have made known your might among the peoples.
ESV.15: You with your arm redeemed your people, the children of Jacob and Joseph. Selah
ESV.16: When the waters saw you, O God, when the waters saw you, they were afraid; indeed, the deep trembled.
ESV.17: The clouds poured out water; the skies gave forth thunder; your arrows flashed on every side.
ESV.18: The crash of your thunder was in the whirlwind; your lightnings lighted up the world; the earth trembled and shook.
ESV.19: Your way was through the sea, your path through the great waters; yet your footprints were unseen.
ESV.20: You led your people like a flock by the hand of Moses and Aaron.

NASB.1: For the choir director; according to Jeduthun. A Psalm of Asaph. My voice [rises] to God, and I will cry aloud; My voice [rises] to God, and He will hear me.
NASB.2: In the day of my trouble I sought the Lord; In the night my hand was stretched out without weariness; My soul refused to be comforted.
NASB.3: [When] I remember God, then I am disturbed; [When] I sigh, then my spirit grows faint. Selah.
NASB.4: You have held my eyelids [open]; I am so troubled that I cannot speak.
NASB.5: I have considered the days of old, The years of long ago.
NASB.6: I will remember my song in the night; I will meditate with my heart, And my spirit ponders:
NASB.7: Will the Lord reject forever? And will He never be favorable again?
NASB.8: Has His lovingkindness ceased forever? Has [His] promise come to an end forever?
NASB.9: Has God forgotten to be gracious, Or has He in anger withdrawn His compassion? Selah.
NASB.10: Then I said, 'It is my grief, That the right hand of the Most High has changed.'
NASB.11: I shall remember the deeds of the LORD; Surely I will remember Your wonders of old.
NASB.12: I will meditate on all Your work And muse on Your deeds.
NASB.13: Your way, O God, is holy; What god is great like our God?
NASB.14: You are the God who works wonders; You have made known Your strength among the peoples.
NASB.15: You have by Your power redeemed Your people, The sons of Jacob and Joseph. Selah.
NASB.16: The waters saw You, O God; The waters saw You, they were in anguish; The deeps also trembled.
NASB.17: The clouds poured out water; The skies gave forth a sound; Your arrows flashed here and there.
NASB.18: The sound of Your thunder was in the whirlwind; The lightnings lit up the world; The earth trembled and shook.
NASB.19: Your way was in the sea And Your paths in the mighty waters, And Your footprints may not be known.
NASB.20: You led Your people like a flock By the hand of Moses and Aaron.

NLT.1:
I cry out to God; yes, I shout.
Oh, that God would listen to me!
NLT.2:
When I was in deep trouble,
I searched for the Lord.
All night long I prayed, with hands lifted toward heaven,
but my soul was not comforted.
NLT.3:
I think of God, and I moan,
overwhelmed with longing for his help. Interlude
NLT.4:
You don’t let me sleep.
I am too distressed even to pray!
NLT.5:
I think of the good old days,
long since ended,
NLT.6:
when my nights were filled with joyful songs.
I search my soul and ponder the difference now.
NLT.7:
Has the Lord rejected me forever?
Will he never again be kind to me?
NLT.8:
Is his unfailing love gone forever?
Have his promises permanently failed?
NLT.9:
Has God forgotten to be gracious?
Has he slammed the door on his compassion? Interlude
NLT.10:
And I said, “This is my fate;
the Most High has turned his hand against me.”
NLT.11:
But then I recall all you have done, O LORD;
I remember your wonderful deeds of long ago.
NLT.12:
They are constantly in my thoughts.
I cannot stop thinking about your mighty works.
NLT.13:
O God, your ways are holy.
Is there any god as mighty as you?
NLT.14:
You are the God of great wonders!
You demonstrate your awesome power among the nations.
NLT.15:
By your strong arm, you redeemed your people,
the descendants of Jacob and Joseph. Interlude
NLT.16:
When the Red Sea saw you, O God,
its waters looked and trembled!
The sea quaked to its very depths.
NLT.17:
The clouds poured down rain;
the thunder rumbled in the sky.
Your arrows of lightning flashed.
NLT.18:
Your thunder roared from the whirlwind;
the lightning lit up the world!
The earth trembled and shook.
NLT.19:
Your road led through the sea,
your pathway through the mighty waters —
a pathway no one knew was there!
NLT.20:
You led your people along that road like a flock of sheep,
with Moses and Aaron as their shepherds.

WEB.1: For the Chief Musician. To Jeduthun. A Psalm by Asaph. My cry goes to God! Indeed, I cry to God for help, and for him to listen to me.
WEB.2: In the day of my trouble I sought the Lord. My hand was stretched out in the night, and didn’t get tired. My soul refused to be comforted.
WEB.3: I remember God, and I groan. I complain, and my spirit is overwhelmed. Selah.
WEB.4: You hold my eyelids open. I am so troubled that I can’t speak.
WEB.5: I have considered the days of old, the years of ancient times.
WEB.6: I remember my song in the night. I consider in my own heart; my spirit diligently inquires:
WEB.7: “Will the Lord reject us forever? Will he be favorable no more?
WEB.8: Has his loving kindness vanished forever? Does his promise fail for generations?
WEB.9: Has God forgotten to be gracious? Has he, in anger, withheld his compassion?” Selah.
WEB.10: Then I thought, “I will appeal to this: the years of the right hand of the Most High.”
WEB.11: I will remember Yah’s deeds; for I will remember your wonders of old.
WEB.12: I will also meditate on all your work, and consider your doings.
WEB.13: Your way, God, is in the sanctuary. What god is great like God?
WEB.14: You are the God who does wonders. You have made your strength known among the peoples.
WEB.15: You have redeemed your people with your arm, the sons of Jacob and Joseph. Selah.
WEB.16: The waters saw you, God. The waters saw you, and they writhed. The depths also convulsed.
WEB.17: The clouds poured out water. The skies resounded with thunder. Your arrows also flashed around.
WEB.18: The voice of your thunder was in the whirlwind. The lightnings lit up the world. The earth trembled and shook.
WEB.19: Your way was through the sea; your paths through the great waters. Your footsteps were not known.
WEB.20: You led your people like a flock, by the hand of Moses and Aaron.

CSB17.1:
For the choir director: according to Jeduthun. Of Asaph. ⓚ A psalm. [1] I cry aloud to God,
aloud to God, and he will hear me. ⓛ
CSB17.2:
I sought the Lord in my day of trouble.
My hands were continually lifted up
all night long;
I refused to be comforted. ⓜ
CSB17.3:
I think of God; I groan;
I meditate; my spirit becomes weak. ⓝ Selah
CSB17.4:
You have kept me from closing my eyes;
I am troubled and cannot speak. ⓐ
CSB17.5:
I consider days of old,
years long past. ⓑ
CSB17.6:
At night I remember my music;
I meditate in my heart, and my spirit ponders. ⓒ
CSB17.7:
“Will the Lord reject forever
and never again show favor? ⓓ
CSB17.8:
Has his faithful love ceased forever?
Is his promise at an end for all generations? ⓔ
CSB17.9:
Has God forgotten to be gracious?
Has he in anger withheld his compassion?” ⓕ Selah
CSB17.10:
So I say, “I am grieved
that the right hand of the Most High has changed.” [1] ⓖ
CSB17.11:
I will remember the LORD’s works;
yes, I will remember your ancient wonders. ⓗ
CSB17.12:
I will reflect on all you have done
and meditate on your actions. ⓘ
CSB17.13:
God, your way is holy.
What god is great like God? ⓙ
CSB17.14:
You are the God who works wonders;
you revealed your strength among the peoples. ⓚ
CSB17.15:
With power you redeemed your people,
the descendants of Jacob and Joseph. ⓛ Selah
CSB17.16:
The water saw you, God.
The water saw you; it trembled.
Even the depths shook. ⓜ
CSB17.17:
The clouds poured down water.
The storm clouds thundered;
your arrows flashed back and forth. ⓝ
CSB17.18:
The sound of your thunder was in the whirlwind;
lightning lit up the world. ⓐ
The earth shook and quaked. ⓑ
CSB17.19:
Your way went through the sea
and your path through the vast water,
but your footprints were unseen. ⓒ
CSB17.20:
You led your people like a flock ⓓ
by the hand of Moses and Aaron. ⓔ

AMP.1:
To the Chief Musician; according to Jeduthun [one of David's three chief musicians, founder of an official musical family]. A Psalm of Asaph. My voice rises to God, and I will cry aloud;
My voice rises to God, and He will hear me.
AMP.2:
In the day of my trouble I [desperately] sought the Lord;
In the night my hand was stretched out [in prayer] without weariness;
My soul refused to be comforted.
AMP.3:
I remember God; then I am disquieted and I groan;
I sigh [in prayer], and my spirit grows faint. Selah.
AMP.4:
You have held my eyelids open;
I am so troubled that I cannot speak.
AMP.5:
I have considered the ancient days,
The years [of prosperity] of long, long ago.
AMP.6:
I will remember my song in the night;
I will meditate with my heart,
And my spirit searches:
AMP.7:
Will the Lord reject forever?
And will He never be favorable again?
AMP.8:
Has His lovingkindness ceased forever?
Have His promises ended for all time?
AMP.9:
Has God forgotten to be gracious?
Or has He in anger withdrawn His compassion? Selah.
AMP.10:
And I said, "This is my grief,
That the right hand of the Most High has changed [and His lovingkindness is withheld]."
AMP.11:
I will [solemnly] remember the deeds of the LORD;
Yes, I will [wholeheartedly] remember Your wonders of old.
AMP.12:
I will meditate on all Your works
And thoughtfully consider all Your [great and wondrous] deeds.
AMP.13:
Your way, O God, is holy [far from sin and guilt].
What god is great like our God?
AMP.14:
You are the [awesome] God who works [powerful] wonders;
You have demonstrated Your power among the people.
AMP.15:
You have with Your [great] arm redeemed Your people,
The sons of Jacob and Joseph. Selah.
AMP.16:
The waters [of the Red Sea] saw You, O God;
The waters saw You, they were in anguish;
The deeps also trembled.
AMP.17:
The clouds poured down water;
The skies sent out a sound [of rumbling thunder];
Your arrows (lightning) flashed here and there.
AMP.18:
The voice of Your thunder was in the whirlwind;
The lightnings illumined the world;
The earth trembled and shook.
AMP.19:
Your way [of escape for Your people] was through the sea,
And Your paths through the great waters,
And Your footprints were not traceable.
AMP.20:
You led Your people like a flock
By the hand of Moses and Aaron [to the promised goal].

MSG.1:
An Asaph psalm
I yell out to my God, I yell with all my might,
I yell at the top of my lungs. He listens.
MSG.2:
I found myself in trouble and went looking for my Lord;
my life was an open wound that wouldn't heal.
When friends said, "Everything will turn out all right,"
I didn't believe a word they said.
MSG.3:
I remember God—and shake my head.
I bow my head—then wring my hands.
MSG.4:
I'm awake all night—not a wink of sleep;
I can't even say what's bothering me.
MSG.5:
I go over the days one by one,
I ponder the years gone by.
MSG.6:
I strum my lute all through the night,
wondering how to get my life together.
MSG.7:
Will the Lord walk off and leave us for good?
Will he never smile again?
MSG.8:
Is his love worn threadbare?
Has his salvation promise burned out?
MSG.9:
Has God forgotten his manners?
Has he angrily stalked off and left us?
MSG.10:
"Just my luck," I said. "The High God goes out of business
just the moment I need him."
MSG.11:
Once again I'll go over what God has done,
lay out on the table the ancient wonders;
MSG.12:
I'll ponder all the things you've accomplished,
and give a long, loving look at your acts.
MSG.13:
O God! Your way is holy!
No god is great like God!
MSG.14:
You're the God who makes things happen;
you showed everyone what you can do—
MSG.15:
You pulled your people out of the worst kind of trouble,
rescued the children of Jacob and Joseph.
MSG.16:
Ocean saw you in action, God,
saw you and trembled with fear;
Deep Ocean was scared to death.
MSG.17:
Clouds belched buckets of rain,
Sky exploded with thunder,
your arrows flashing this way and that.
MSG.18:
From Whirlwind came your thundering voice,
Lightning exposed the world,
Earth reeled and rocked.
MSG.19:
You strode right through Ocean,
walked straight through roaring Ocean,
but nobody saw you come or go.
MSG.20:
Hidden in the hands of Moses and Aaron,
You led your people like a flock of sheep.

NIV.1:
Psalm 77
For the director of music. For Jeduthun. Of Asaph. A psalm.
I cried out to God for help;
I cried out to God to hear me.
NIV.2:
When I was in distress, I sought the Lord;
at night I stretched out untiring hands
and my soul refused to be comforted.
NIV.3:
I remembered you, O God, and I groaned;
I mused, and my spirit grew faint. Selah
NIV.4:
You kept my eyes from closing;
I was too troubled to speak.
NIV.5:
I thought about the former days,
the years of long ago;
NIV.6:
I remembered my songs in the night.
My heart mused and my spirit inquired:
NIV.7:
“Will the Lord reject forever?
Will he never show his favor again?
NIV.8:
Has his unfailing love vanished forever?
Has his promise failed for all time?
NIV.9:
Has God forgotten to be merciful?
Has he in anger withheld his compassion?” Selah
NIV.10:
Then I thought, “To this I will appeal:
the years of the right hand of the Most High.”
NIV.11:
I will remember the deeds of the Lord;
yes, I will remember your miracles of long ago.
NIV.12:
I will meditate on all your works
and consider all your mighty deeds.
NIV.13:
Your ways, O God, are holy.
What god is so great as our God?
NIV.14:
You are the God who performs miracles;
you display your power among the peoples.
NIV.15:
With your mighty arm you redeemed your people,
the descendants of Jacob and Joseph. Selah
NIV.16:
The waters saw you, O God,
the waters saw you and writhed;
the very depths were convulsed.
NIV.17:
The clouds poured down water,
the skies resounded with thunder;
your arrows flashed back and forth.
NIV.18:
Your thunder was heard in the whirlwind,
your lightning lit up the world;
the earth trembled and quaked.
NIV.19:
Your path led through the sea,
your way through the mighty waters,
though your footprints were not seen.
NIV.20:
You led your people like a flock
by the hand of Moses and Aaron.

NRSVCE.1:
I cry aloud to God,
aloud to God, that he may hear me.
NRSVCE.2:
In the day of my trouble I seek the Lord;
in the night my hand is stretched out without wearying;
my soul refuses to be comforted.
NRSVCE.3:
I think of God, and I moan;
I meditate, and my spirit faints. Selah
NRSVCE.4:
You keep my eyelids from closing;
I am so troubled that I cannot speak.
NRSVCE.5:
I consider the days of old,
and remember the years of long ago.
NRSVCE.6:
I commune with my heart in the night;
I meditate and search my spirit:
NRSVCE.7:
“Will the Lord spurn forever,
and never again be favorable?
NRSVCE.8:
Has his steadfast love ceased forever?
Are his promises at an end for all time?
NRSVCE.9:
Has God forgotten to be gracious?
Has he in anger shut up his compassion?” Selah
NRSVCE.10:
And I say, “It is my grief
that the right hand of the Most High has changed.”
NRSVCE.11:
I will call to mind the deeds of the LORD;
I will remember your wonders of old.
NRSVCE.12:
I will meditate on all your work,
and muse on your mighty deeds.
NRSVCE.13:
Your way, O God, is holy.
What god is so great as our God?
NRSVCE.14:
You are the God who works wonders;
you have displayed your might among the peoples.
NRSVCE.15:
With your strong arm you redeemed your people,
the descendants of Jacob and Joseph. Selah
NRSVCE.16:
When the waters saw you, O God,
when the waters saw you, they were afraid;
the very deep trembled.
NRSVCE.17:
The clouds poured out water;
the skies thundered;
your arrows flashed on every side.
NRSVCE.18:
The crash of your thunder was in the whirlwind;
your lightnings lit up the world;
the earth trembled and shook.
NRSVCE.19:
Your way was through the sea,
your path, through the mighty waters;
yet your footprints were unseen.
NRSVCE.20:
You led your people like a flock
by the hand of Moses and Aaron.

NET.1: I will cry out to God and call for help! I will cry out to God and he will pay attention to me.
NET.2: In my time of trouble I sought the Lord. I kept my hand raised in prayer throughout the night. I refused to be comforted.
NET.3: I said, “I will remember God while I groan; I will think about him while my strength leaves me.” (Selah)
NET.4: You held my eyelids open; I was troubled and could not speak.
NET.5: I thought about the days of old, about ancient times.
NET.6: I said, “During the night I will remember the song I once sang; I will think very carefully.” I tried to make sense of what was happening.
NET.7: I asked, “Will the Lord reject me forever? Will he never again show me his favor?
NET.8: Has his loyal love disappeared forever? Has his promise failed forever?
NET.9: Has God forgotten to be merciful? Has his anger stifled his compassion?”
NET.10: Then I said, “I am sickened by the thought that the sovereign One might become inactive.
NET.11: I will remember the works of the LORD. Yes, I will remember the amazing things you did long ago!
NET.12: I will think about all you have done; I will reflect upon your deeds!”
NET.13: O God, your deeds are extraordinary! What god can compare to our great God?
NET.14: You are the God who does amazing things; you have revealed your strength among the nations.
NET.15: You delivered your people by your strength — the children of Jacob and Joseph. (Selah)
NET.16: The waters saw you, O God, the waters saw you and trembled. Yes, the depths of the sea shook with fear.
NET.17: The clouds poured down rain; the skies thundered. Yes, your arrows flashed about.
NET.18: Your thunderous voice was heard in the wind; the lightning bolts lit up the world; the earth trembled and shook.
NET.19: You walked through the sea; you passed through the surging waters, but left no footprints.
NET.20: You led your people like a flock of sheep, by the hand of Moses and Aaron.
